cases.title: Testing-plan policy-dependent rows consume single BR-20 effective policy at check-in and close
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 coverage blocks for GPS validation, Attendance status assignment, and Close-time absent finalization apply.
Hierarchy fixtures exist: institution, faculty, course, and class-section policies with distinct field values.
Open class session exists for the target section with enrolled students and valid QR token.
steps: Invoke M06 resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionScheduledStart) and record resolved presentWindowMinutes, lateWindowMinutes, gpsRequired, and manualEditWindowHours with per-field source levels.
Submit student check-in within resolved present window and verify Present assignment (AC-11 row).
Submit check-in with resolved gpsRequired true and missing GPS payload; verify GpsRequired rejection (AC-09 row).
Close session with pending students; verify Absent finalization applies per resolved policy at close (AC-12 row).
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix cross-walk confirms BR-20 produces one merged effective policy feeding all testing-plan policy-dependent rows: check-in status windows, GPS enforcement, and close-time absent processing each consume the same precedence-resolved policy.

cases.title: M06 resolveEffectivePolicy merges per-field precedence section over course over faculty over institution
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for attendance_policies table.
Institution policy: presentWindowMinutes 10, lateWindowMinutes 10, gpsRequired false, manualEditWindowHours 24.
Faculty policy: presentWindowMinutes 12 (other fields undefined).
Course policy: lateWindowMinutes 18, gpsRequired true, gpsRadiusMeters 120 (presentWindowMinutes undefined).
Class-section policy: presentWindowMinutes 20 only (other fields undefined).
Target classSectionId belongs to the configured hierarchy.
steps: Seed four active AttendancePolicy rows at Institution, Faculty, Course, and ClassSection scopes.
Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionTimestamp) from M06 Policy Engine.
Assert presentWindowMinutes 20 sourced from ClassSection.
Assert lateWindowMinutes 18 sourced from Course (section and faculty undefined).
Assert gpsRequired true and gpsRadiusMeters 120 sourced from Course.
Assert manualEditWindowHours 24 sourced from Institution (undefined at all child levels).
cases.expected: BR-20 produces one merged effective policy: each field uses the most specific defined value (section > course > faculty > institution); undefined fields fall back to parent levels without requiring duplicate configuration at every scope.

cases.title: M06 resolves identical effective policy at check-in timestamp and session close for same class section
preconditions: Class section has section-scoped policy with presentWindowMinutes 15, lateWindowMinutes 15, and autoCloseEnabled true.
Open class session exists with known scheduledStart and classSectionId.
M04 check-in pipeline and M03 session-close hook both call resolveEffectivePolicy.
steps: Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, checkInTimestamp) during an open session and capture resolved policy snapshot.
Process a successful check-in and verify Present/Late decision uses the captured present and late window values.
Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionCloseTimestamp) from M03 close processing.
Compare close-time snapshot to check-in snapshot for presentWindowMinutes, lateWindowMinutes, autoCloseEnabled, and manualEditWindowHours.
Verify auto-absent finalization (BR-13) and auto-close evaluation (BR-21) reference the close-time resolved policy.
cases.expected: BR-20 mandates resolution at check-in validation, session open/close, and manual edit authorization; for the same section and session, effective policy fields are consistent unless an admin mutates policy between events.

cases.title: Manual edit authorization uses BR-20 resolved manualEditWindowHours at session close time
preconditions: Institution default manualEditWindowHours 24; section-scoped override manualEditWindowHours 72.
Closed session exists with known closedAt timestamp.
Lecturer is assigned to the section.
steps: Capture beforeState: resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, closedAt) shows manualEditWindowHours 72 from section.
Lecturer attempts PATCH attendance correction at closedAt + 48 hours (within 72-hour resolved window).
Assert correction is accepted per BR-14 using resolved edit window, not institution default 24.
Lecturer attempts correction at closedAt + 80 hours (outside 72-hour resolved window).
Assert rejection with EditWindowExpired per BR-15.
cases.expected: beforeState Closed with resolved manualEditWindowHours 72 governs lecturer edit authorization; corrections within 72 hours succeed; beyond 72 hours are rejected; institution default 24 does not apply when section override exists.

cases.title: Institution-only default policy resolves when no faculty, course, or section overrides exist
preconditions: Class section exists with no faculty, course, or section AttendancePolicy rows.
Single institution-scoped policy defines presentWindowMinutes 10, lateWindowMinutes 15, gpsRequired false.
Open session scheduled for the section.
steps: Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionTimestamp).
Assert all behavioral fields match institution policy values with source level Institution.
Submit check-in at scheduledStart + 8 minutes; verify Present.
Submit check-in for second student at scheduledStart + 12 minutes; verify Late.
Confirm no null or error returned at any resolution point.
cases.expected: BR-20 resolves institution defaults as the effective policy when no more specific overrides exist; check-in Present/Late boundaries follow institution presentWindowMinutes 10 and lateWindowMinutes 15 without requiring child-scope rows.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins assigns Present using section-resolved presentWindowMinutes overriding institution default
preconditions: Institution default presentWindowMinutes 10; section-scoped override presentWindowMinutes 25 via POST /v1/policies.
Lecturer has opened session; valid qrToken is available.
Two authenticated enrolled students have no prior success for the session.
Session scheduledStart is known.
steps: Student A calls POST /v1/check-ins with clientTimestamp at scheduledStart + 12 minutes.
Assert HTTP 200, data.outcome Success, data.attendanceStatus Present (section window 25 applies, not institution 10).
Student B calls POST /v1/check-ins with clientTimestamp at scheduledStart + 22 minutes.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.attendanceStatus Present for Student B confirming section override governs outcome.
cases.expected: Effective policy resolution at check-in uses section presentWindowMinutes 25 per BR-20; students checking in between minute 10 and 25 receive Present rather than Late; HTTP 200 success envelope includes correct attendanceStatus.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close finalizes Absent per BR-20 effective policy at close time
preconditions: Open session with five enrolled students: two Present, one Late, two Pending.
Section-scoped policy defines standard absent finalization; no Excused students among pending.
Lecturer assigned to section is authenticated.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with Lecturer Authorization and Idempotency-Key.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.state Closed and data.summary.absent reflecting two previously Pending students.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and verify pending students now show attendanceStatus Absent.
Confirm Present and Late students retain prior statuses.
cases.expected: Session close HTTP 200 response includes absent summary count driven by BR-20 effective policy at close; pending enrolled students without success become Absent per BR-13; existing Present/Late records unchanged.

cases.title: WF-03 student check-in happy path uses BR-20 resolved effective policy for Present assignment
preconditions: WF-01 complete: section, enrollments, sessions Scheduled; section policy override presentWindowMinutes 15.
Lecturer completes WF-02: POST /class-sessions/{id}/open transitions session to Open with QR.
Authenticated enrolled student has no prior success; check-in occurs within resolved present window.
steps: Resolve effective policy for class section at session scheduled start and record presentWindowMinutes.
Student scans QR and POST /v1/check-ins succeeds within present window per WF-03.
Verify WF-03 checkpoint: status assignment Present or Late is derived from effective policy windows.
Confirm CheckInAttempt outcome Success and AttendanceRecord method QR are persisted.
cases.expected: WF-03 primary lifecycle completes with Present status determined by BR-20 effective policy resolution at check-in time; AC-11 checkpoint satisfied via precedence chain section > course > faculty > institution.

cases.title: Per-field fallback: section defines presentWindow only; lateWindow and GPS inherit from course level
preconditions: Institution default: presentWindowMinutes 10, lateWindowMinutes 10, gpsRequired false.
Course policy: lateWindowMinutes 20, gpsRequired true, gpsRadiusMeters 100 (presentWindowMinutes undefined).
Section policy: presentWindowMinutes 30 only (lateWindowMinutes and gpsRequired undefined).
Open session exists for the section with room coordinates configured.
steps: Resolve effective policy and confirm presentWindowMinutes 30 (section), lateWindowMinutes 20 (course), gpsRequired true (course).
Student checks in at scheduledStart + 25 minutes; assert Present (within section present window 30).
Student checks in at scheduledStart + 35 minutes without GPS; assert HTTP 422 with error.code GpsRequired (inherited from course).
Student checks in at scheduledStart + 35 minutes with valid in-radius GPS; assert Late (after present window 30, within late window ending at +50).
cases.expected: Alternate precedence branch: section overrides present window only; late window and GPS requirement inherit from course per BR-20 per-field fallback; check-in outcomes reflect merged effective policy not any single scope row alone.

cases.title: Flow C audit verification after section policy PATCH changes subsequent BR-20 resolution
preconditions: Open session with section policy presentWindowMinutes 15.
Student A successfully checked in Present under 15-minute window.
AcademicAdmin is authenticated; audit query endpoint GET /v1/audit-logs is available.
steps: Record beforeState: resolveEffectivePolicy shows presentWindowMinutes 15.
AcademicAdmin PATCH /v1/policies/{policyId} changing presentWindowMinutes to 30.
Record afterState: resolveEffectivePolicy shows presentWindowMinutes 30.
Student B checks in at scheduledStart + 20 minutes; assert Present (would have been Late under beforeState).
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for policy mutation with old and new presentWindowMinutes values.
Verify Student A AttendanceRecord remains Present unchanged.
cases.expected: Critical policy configuration change updates BR-20 resolution for subsequent check-ins immediately; prior attendance records are immutable; audit trail captures policy mutation per BR-22 with actor, old value, and new value.

cases.title: Student denied policy read and export; cross-scope Lecturer denied effective-policy for unassigned section
preconditions: Student and Lecturer test accounts exist with valid bearer tokens.
Attendance policies exist at institution and section levels for section A.
Lecturer-A is assigned to section A; Lecturer-B is not assigned to section A.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report and policy access.
steps: Student calls GET /v1/policies or effective-policy endpoint.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden; no policy configuration or precedence details leaked.
Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage per BR-18.
Lecturer-B calls effective-policy read for section A; assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope per BR-19.
Lecturer-A calls effective-policy read for assigned section A; assert HTTP 200 with resolved values only.
cases.expected: RBAC enforces AttendancePolicy.read Deny for Student; Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied effective-policy read per BR-19; assigned Lecturer receives resolved values without full policy administration APIs.

cases.title: GPS rejection uses course-level gpsRequired from BR-20 merged policy not institution default
preconditions: Institution default gpsRequired false.
Course-scoped policy: gpsRequired true, gpsRadiusMeters 100.
No section override for gpsRequired.
Open session exists with room coordinates; valid qrToken available.
Authenticated enrolled student has no prior success.
steps: Resolve effective policy and confirm gpsRequired true sourced from Course per BR-20.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken but without GPS coordinates.
Assert HTTP 422 with error.code GpsRequired in response envelope.
Query attendance_records and confirm student remains Pending with failed CheckInAttempt logged.
cases.expected: BR-20 merged policy applies course-level gpsRequired true despite institution default false; missing GPS payload is rejected with GpsRequired per AC-09; no Present/Late AttendanceRecord is created.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: DuplicateCheckIn before GPS checks when gpsRequired resolved true
preconditions: Open session exists with course-level gpsRequired true per BR-20 merged policy.
Enrolled student already has Present or Late AttendanceRecord for the session.
Duplicate submission payload includes GPS coordinates outside allowed radius or omits GPS entirely.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins as already-checked-in student with GPS payload outside configured radius.
Assert error.code is DuplicateCheckIn, not OutOfRadius.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as same student with GPS omitted on GPS-required session.
Assert error.code is DuplicateCheckIn, not GpsDisabled or GpsRequired.
Verify original AttendanceRecord unchanged after both attempts.
cases.expected: Validation order per API design §5.1: duplicate-success check (BR-07 step 5) short-circuits before BR-08 to BR-10 GPS evaluations even when BR-20 resolves gpsRequired true from course-level policy.

cases.title: Idempotent replay and concurrent check-ins use consistent BR-20 policy snapshot
preconditions: Open session with section-scoped policy presentWindowMinutes 15.
Student has completed one successful POST /v1/check-ins with Idempotency-Key K1.
Ten additional enrolled students with no prior success are available for concurrent submission.
steps: Replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key K1, same actor, and same qrToken.
Assert HTTP 200 with prior success summary without second AttendanceRecord per BR-07.
Submit duplicate with new Idempotency-Key K2 and fresh valid qrToken; assert 409 DuplicateCheckIn.
Fire ten concurrent POST /v1/check-ins from distinct students within present window.
Verify all successful outcomes used presentWindowMinutes 15 from stable BR-20 resolution.
cases.expected: BR-07 idempotency replay returns prior accepted result; new-key duplicate returns 409; concurrent check-ins during stable policy configuration all resolve the same effective policy with no split-brain window application.

cases.title: PolicyResolutionSummary accordion displays BR-20 precedence chain and per-field source levels
preconditions: AcademicAdmin logged in on desktop Chromium at /admin/policies create or edit route.
Institution, course, and section policies exist with different presentWindowMinutes values.
PolicyResolutionSummary (DC-09) renders for target class section.
steps: Navigate to FRM-07 policy form for a class section with partial override.
Expand PolicyResolutionSummary accordion sections for present window, late window, GPS required, and manual-edit window.
Verify each field shows resolved effective value and source level badge (section, course, faculty, or institution).
Confirm precedence order displayed as section → course → faculty → institution.
Change section present window value and observe summary preview updates before save.
cases.expected: DC-09 browser journey satisfies BR-20 explainability: admins see single effective policy with precedence chain and per-field provenance before and during configuration.

cases.title: Lecturer live roster shows Present and Late badges driven by BR-20 effective policy windows
preconditions: Lecturer logged in on desktop Chromium.
Open session with section policy presentWindowMinutes 15; students have checked in within present and late windows.
Lecturer navigates to session roster page for assigned section.
steps: Open live roster view for open session and verify SessionStatusBadge shows Open.
Confirm DataTable columns include student name, attendance status badge, check-in time, and method.
Verify students who checked in within 15-minute present window display Present badge.
Verify students who checked in after present window display Late badge.
Observe roster counts update without full page reload when a new student checks in within policy window.
cases.expected: Lecturer UI reflects BR-20 effective policy at runtime: Present/Late badges and roster counts match policy-resolved windows; live updates align with GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance data per FR-19.
